behavior_summary: Provides NPC guard behavior: a simple inspection response (U8P_GUARD5::look), area-enter and scheduling logic to restore/activate the guard (U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea, U8P_GUARD5::schedule), and a conversation handler exposing a removable-question menu with world-state dependent replies (U8P_GUARD5::use).
U8P_GUARD5::look: when the player inspects the guard, the script checks Npc::isDead(this) and returns either a short bark 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman'.

U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea: on a fast-area (trigger) entry, if the NPC is dead it clears dead/in-combat state and teleports the NPC to a fixed map/coordinates; if not dead it calls engine activation methods with suspension points (likely to start AI/scheduling).

U8P_GUARD5::schedule: initializes scheduling parameters, calls an engine scheduling routine, suspends while waiting for the engine result, and on success clears the dead flag and finalizes activation (respawn) via another engine method.

U8P_GUARD5::use: a full conversation flow that sets guard-specific flags (guard5Met, guard5Pissed, guard5Thanked), composes a question menu (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye), removes options as asked, and chooses urgent/panic or location-specific replies based on world flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree, devonInRule).
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD5::look branches immediately on Npc::isDead(this) to select one of two short barks then suspends.

U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea branches on Npc::isDead(this): dead -> Npc::clrDead / Npc::clrInCombat / Npc::teleport; alive -> METHOD::func091C, suspend, METHOD::func15E7 with suspends between (asynchronous activation sequence).
U8P_GUARD5::schedule sets four temporary locals, calls METHOD::func143A, suspends, then tests process_result() and on true clears dead flag and calls METHOD::func133F before suspending again.

U8P_GUARD5::use opens a conversation loop: begins conversation, may run spawn/free housekeeping, builds local2 menu array, Ask -> suspend to capture choice into local1, strcmp on local1 to dispatch replies, MenuRemove to avoid repeating options, and sets/writes guard flags during branches until the player picks 'Bye' to end.
open_questions: Exact semantics of METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A, and METHOD::func133F (engine internals: likely scheduling/AI activation but exact effects are unknown).
Precise meaning of the scheduling numeric locals (local0..local3) and how they parameterize METHOD::func143A (values are present but their mapping is inferred).
Low-confidence spawn/free interactions in U8P_GUARD5::use (FREE::func1C19 and FREE::resetRef) — their exact purpose (resource ref reset, pid allocation, or other housekeeping) is unclear.
Randomized reply behavior's distribution and any hidden side effects (urandom branch not fully detailed).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD5::look
main_function.purpose: Return a short inspection description for the guard: report 'dead guardsman' if Npc::isDead(this) is true, otherwise 'guardsman'.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player chooses to inspect ('U8P_GUARD5::look' at) the NPC.
Runs as a U8P_GUARD5::look/inspect process (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::look
function_map.role: Handles player 'U8P_GUARD5::look' inspection; shows appropriate bark depending on Npc::isDead(this).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: restore/resurrect NPC if dead (clrDead/clrInCombat/teleport) or start activation sequence for alive NPC via engine methods.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::schedule
function_map.role: Scheduling/respawn helper: initialize parameters, call engine scheduling, await process_result(), then clear dead flag and finalize activation on success.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD5::use
function_map.role: Conversation handler: begin conversation, build and present question menu, branch on choices, update guard-related flags, and end conversation.

external_calls.purpose: Test whether the guard is dead to choose the U8P_GUARD5::look/bark response.
Branch on alive vs dead behavior when a trigger area is entered.
Clear the NPC's dead flag to respawn/restore it.
Clear in-combat state so the NPC appears non-hostile after restore.
Move the NPC to map 0x29 at coordinates (100,100) when restoring from dead state.
Engine/AI activation helper invoked in alive branch before suspend (likely start/queue AI behavior).
Follow-up engine/AI helper invoked after a suspend (likely continued activation).
Register or queue a scheduled process with the engine using prepared parameters.
Check the asynchronous result of the scheduled engine call to decide whether to clear dead flag and finalize activation.
Clear the NPC dead flag when scheduling completes successfully (respawn).
Finalize activation/respawn after clearing the dead flag (engine post‑processing).
Appears to interact with spawn/free subsystem (low-confidence: possibly obtain pid or touch spawn refs) during conversation start.
Housekeeping on a spawn/free reference (reset a ref id) during conversation start.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a temporary counter used to choose urgent/panic dialogue; indicates Hydros Titan freed.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to urgent-dialogue counter and selects specific urgent line; indicates another world-state condition.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to urgent-dialogue counter; indicates Pyros Titan freed.

reads.name: guard5Met
reads.likely_effect: Selects met vs first-time greeting in U8P_GUARD5::use.

reads.name: guard5Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Gates an annoyed short reply branch; read to choose annoyed vs neutral responses.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used to choose location-specific replies and to gate a 'thank you' sequence referencing a character removal.

reads.name: guard5Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read (negated) to avoid repeating the thank-you sequence.

writes.name: guard5Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player first talks to the guard to mark he has been met.

writes.name: guard5Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set to 0 in a clearing branch and set to 1 in a rude reply branch (tracks guard annoyance).

writes.name: guard5Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the guard expresses gratitude (prevents repeating the thanks).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides NPC guard behavior: a simple inspection response (U8P_GUARD5::look), area-enter and scheduling logic to restore/activate the guard (U8P_GUARD5::enterFastArea, U8P_GUARD5::schedule), and a conversation handler exposing a removable-question menu with world-state dependent replies (U8P_GUARD5::use).
